content:
Hi! I tried madness w the following teams usually using carry with scaling,

1) Pollux (AA), faint, aigis, saya
2) Daffodil (AA), thais, aigis, faint
3) Clem (AA), saya, thais, faint

What are boons? Relics?

Thanks!
Relics you get within the run. What I call "boons" ( didn't have access to the game when I wrote that ) are Little N's help. How many of these did you unlock ?

And your teams seem fine ( Especially Daff if you can feed her ).
Did you upgrade their skills levels ? Soulforge ?

( And rereading you post I see you did unlock Wheels A-Rolling. Nice farming within only a month ! )

What WoDs are you on ?

<@767916775001292820> Heyo : )

Any specific advice on the Pollux linup for Madness train ?
If you want an easier time make sure to upgrade your tree to a good amount <:Saya_caught:1513123193341349968>
I'd prefer someone like Winkle or Erica since Faint Weakness unreliable since it's on Devour and you're on PCaro
Relics that increase Base Damage is really good since Pollux damage scales off of it really well
As for Clem

Clementine guides:
Feel free to ping @spooky_penguin_ for clem related qns

Thanks <:Sorel_bowing:1356053126247092476>

I think I had about 5-6 when I reached 1-6 boss fight. Should I go for all the silvee/gold relics even with symptoms? I read a guide before saying no symptoms > relics unless the relic is extremely worth it. I will buyout the relic shop if I can. Problem is, I cant do enough damage nor survive the first boss fight. Am I missing something?

The more symptoms on your deck, the less arithmetica and useful skills available each turn.
That aside, some relics are more than worth it so it's up to you.
That's less of an issue when you play discard u.u

Still, I usually prefer black sigils to look for and buy portraits, especially since you can only carry a limited amount of relics and you get 1 for free + 1 after each boss

Understood 
And I asked for your opinion 
You seem knowledgeable enough to the point your opinion seems mandatory to follow which is what I’m going to do
It’s seem like I’ll be giving up on pontos for now and focuson the 2 new ones in the triple banner after that return to saya and here Wheel

On that point I agree Trying things out is better than just asking but I don’t have that much time for it so wanted to stick to some teams that are guaranteed to work but know I think that’s wrong, on should try it all I enjoy it until one hits a hurdle forcing me to reconsider my plans

Actually I did think that since I have some from every realm I should focus on getting some WoDs since I have quite a few of them  
Thank you very much for your time and help
The first chapters are really forgiving so going through the story mode should let you experience quite a few linups along the way without getting too frustrating.
My pleasure 😊

I keep running out of damage in the last phase from factors like stacking fortress. Skill issue? I'm not sure what to do anymore. My current team is castor jenkins horla ogier. If anyone could give some tips, I'd love that

all my characters are in the image.
Pull pollux and play him with castor
Pollux Castor Horla Aigis
Does he need e3? Or would e0 be fine? I have zero pulls rn and won’t get much more unless a crazy value pack shows up
At least e1 but ideally e3
😭 oh
Well thanks
This is my team btw,
Should I choose all three characters or just one? If I only choose one, which one is the easiest to play?

Hello there and welcome aboard : )

All three are great.
- Helot:Catena is quite straightfoward and brings lots of damage
- Pollux specialises in bleed and has his own draw mechanic which does not compete with other ultra's leap or quasar
- Corposant is a potent addition to any discard team

Did you take some time to try linups from all 4 realms ?
I only have a basic understanding of the Caro faction; I don't understand the other three factions very well.<:Helot_slack_off:1459092159465521184>

Okay. Did you find the trials under the "records" button ? There you can try each and every awakener, that would give you a first taste.

Beyond that, it's all about giving it a try : grab an awakener you like, throw in 3 more and keep that linup for a dozen fights.
By then you should have answered some questions and any other you can ask here : )
Did you find Cheri's handbook ?

[AT LEAST 160 FREE STANDARD PULLS]

Go to the thread below for helpful Beginner guides and Redemption codes 

https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1509396840330825758/1509396840330825758

I'll leave you to it then. Quick advices :
- Chaos are pretty versatile and work well with death resistance if younuse a pure chaos linup
- Æquor take some time to build up so maybe don't spam the raging waves until you're satisfied with the tentacle count
- Caro need a strong healer / shielder but you should also try to keep yur health low as much as you can
- Most ultra revolve around playing the very first card of a round. The annilation gimmick can be somewhat confusing at first so you needn't hesitate to ask for help regarding a specific linup. Basically : don't put two awakeners that use leap or quasar together
I'll leave you to it, I stand by if you have more questions
Could Ultra help me win quickly if I understand how to use it properly?

It could, yeah. 
The ultra space is that set of cards you okay first within 5 turns. After that, an extra "ultra round" start.
Some, Like Daffodil or Arachne, allow you to tweat this mechanic a bit
The main issue about ultra is their durability : there are a few versatile units ( Horla, Erica, Clementine, ... ) very very few adepts at shielding / healing

So throwing in Celeste or Sanga could help keeping them alive.
Or you go caro with Aigis/Faint

And if you're interested in shuffle, there's always the Cassiah-Corpo combo although it's not really easy to pull off
ok thanks <:Celeste_agree:1400771815076528258>
Also depends on the symptom. Claustrophobia or trauma for exemple can actually get useful on occasions. Somniloquy is a hard pass as it multiplies.
Finally, if you expect a run with a few symptoms there is a wheel that actually benefits from it ( SR memory syndrome )
I see ty. Any recommended team comp for the train this season?
Thank you!!

Additionally, we have a triple banner right now featuring three powerful units.
- Helot:Catera deals heavy damage in caro but need support ( shield / heal + strength )
- Pollux is speciallized in bleed and does not compete with other ultra for leap or quasar
- Corposant is a strong shielder that really shines in discard linups
Rn im running corpo saya aurita and idk who should i use for fourth slot
